hero.title:
I design interfaces
that do what you expect.
hero.sub: Master's student in Human-Computer Interaction, holding a bachelor's degree in Information Sciences from Utrecht University. My research focuses on deceptive patterns and habitual behaviour in digital environments; alongside my studies I work as Senior Sales Associate and Daily Operations Lead at Amac.
hero.honest: This site uses no cookies, no trackers and no deceptive patterns, the latter happens to be my field of research ;).

highlights.title: At a glance
highlights.lead: Four moments that best summarise my profile.

about.title: Where people and technology meet
paragraphs: I'm Robert Karzijn. My interest lies at the intersection of people and technology: how people use interfaces, which habits form along the way, and how design (deliberately or not) plays into that. In my bachelor's thesis I experimentally investigated how web shops can disrupt procedural memory by relocating interface elements.

Alongside my studies I work at Amac in Apeldoorn, where I progressed from Junior Sales Associate to Daily Operations Lead in two years and worked across several branches to share knowledge and experience. That combination keeps me sharp: theory on user behaviour in the lecture hall, and real users in front of me on the shop floor every day.

I'm currently pursuing a master's in Human-Computer Interaction, focusing on cognitive UX and ethical interface design. Outside study and work: photography, audio and the occasional evening of gaming.

projects.title: From research to the stage
projects.lead: Three projects that show how I work: empirical research, designing with real stakeholders, and organising against a deadline.

experience.title: My route so far
experience.lead: Study and work deliberately run in parallel for me: research and theory at university, responsibility in practice.

footer.note: Built without frameworks, cookies or deceptive patterns.
